title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS system that converts 10,000+ Obsidian notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture of raw content, indexing, and a Wiki layer to enable efficient deep research and retrieval without requiring vector or graph databases.
writeup: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS that turns more than 10,000 Obsidian notes, documents, and repositories into structured context for AI agents. The system relies on a three-layer architecture: a raw content layer, an indexing layer, and a Wiki layer. Its notable claim is enabling deep research and retrieval through file-based indexing, without vector or graph databases. The talk covers context management, the semantic layer, and RAG as applied to a large personal knowledge base.
